steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content: For the system to have no solution, line $k$ must be parallel to line $h$ but not the same line.

steps.title: Check the listed t-values
steps.content:
From the graph, line $h$ crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,-8)$. For line $k$, setting $x=0$ in $sx+40y=t$ gives:
$$40y=t$$
If $t=-320$, then $y=-8$, so line $k$ would have the same $y$-intercept as line $h$. With the required parallel slope, that would make the two lines identical, giving infinitely many solutions instead of no solution.
Therefore $t=-320$ is not possible, so D is correct.
